comment.body:
What Makes a Negotiation Genius?
A negotiation genius isn’t born with innate talents but rather develops specific skills through systematic preparation and strategic execution. According to Malhotra and Bazerman, a negotiation genius is someone who can consistently negotiate successful deals while preserving their integrity and strengthening relationships. These individuals don’t rely on gut instincts or improvisation but instead prepare thoroughly and systematically to achieve consistently superior outcomes. The authors emphasize that successful negotiation isn’t about intuition but rather careful preparation and sound strategy implementation. They define the ultimate goal of negotiation not simply as achieving win-win outcomes but creating value so both sides are better off with an agreement than without it.
The foundation of negotiation genius rests on three core components: a comprehensive toolbox of strategies, a solid conceptual framework, and a sound psychological approach to the negotiation process. By mastering these elements, anyone can develop the skills necessary to navigate even the most challenging negotiation scenarios with confidence and success.

Need to find time for the book

comment.body:
Let me try to answer the questions.
1) the relationship between volatility and liquidity is inverse. High volatility typically means low liquidity, and vice versa. This is because when liquidity is high,  bidders can easily meet sellers, leading to narrower spreads and lower volatility. On the other hand, when liquidity is low, bidders may struggle to find sellers, resulting in wider spreads and higher volatility.
2) a negative speed profile means shorter gamma on the upside and longer gamma on the downside. When spot moves up, customers are bidding which makes MM shorter delta. To stay delta-neutral, MM has to buy futures or options, as a result, more bidding and more demand increases volatility. Conversely, if spot moves down, the longer gamma forces MM to sell futures or options to maintain delta-neutral. the force of sellers increases and bids are easily filled, resulting higher liquidity and lower volatility.

2) a negative speed profile means shorter gamma on the upside and longer gamma on the downside. When spot moves up, customers are bidding which makes MM shorter delta. To stay delta-neutral, MM has to buy futures or options, as a result, more bidding and more demand increases volatility. Conversely, if spot moves down, the longer gamma forces MM to sell futures or options to maintain delta-neutral. the force of sellers increases and bids are easily filled, resulting higher liquidity and lower volatility.

comment.body:
One better to listen to Ray Dalio. 
A brilliant mind in the industry, and not a permabear. No bear/bull duality pls.
Only bcs somebody sees the weaknesses of a system, doesn't make him bear or bull. But makes him possible to spot out weaknesses and strenghts, over- or underpricings. 
He was able to become a billionare. I also learned a lot from his writings. I even have his books. worth reading
